accepted.reason: Cross-distance computation is a reusable dense numeric kernel with clear vectorized loop structure.
Feature binning/statistics loops are practical ML preprocessing kernels for optimization traces.
N-body pairwise force accumulation is a classic high-value performance workload.
Particle aggregation and radial-distance computations provide reusable simulation data kernels.
Soil heat flux/divergence loops are strong stencil-like numeric operations.
2D propagation update loops are highly suitable for Cython conversion and optimization training.
Dynamic-programming sequence alignment is a canonical algorithmic kernel with rich loop structure.

skipped.reason: Integer codec internals are wire-format specific and less transferable for general optimization supervision.
CUDA stream backend bindings are runtime integration glue rather than standalone compute kernels.
UTF-8 parsing internals are specialized low-level text codec logic outside core numeric optimization goals.
